question: what is an animal
answer: An animal is any member of the kingdom Animalia, which is defined as multicellular eukaryotic organisms that have a well-defined shape and usually limited growth, can move voluntarily, actively acquire food and digest it internally, and have sensory and nervous systems that allow them to respond rapidly to stimuli (https://www.dictionary.com/browse/animal). They are heterotrophic in nature (https://www.microscopemaster.com/kingdom-animalia.html), meaning they cannot produce their own food and must consume other organisms or organic matter to survive. Animals can be found in a wide range of shapes, sizes, and forms, and can be classified into different phyla based on their characteristics, such as the presence of a backbone (chordata), the presence of a exoskeleton (arthropoda, mollusca, echinodermata), or the absence of a backbone (sponges, cnidaria, platyhelminthes, nematoda, annelids) (https://www.online-sciences.com/biology/general-characteristics-of-kingdom-animalia-phylum-arthropoda-mollusca-echinodermata-chordata/).
